root_cause_analyses.proximate_cause: Info.plist content read as raw string without XML validation (line 476)
root_cause_analyses.root_cause: Privacy compliance check uses string matching instead of proper XML parsing. The check 'key not in info_plist' (line 485) operates on raw text, not parsed XML structure. This means empty files, malformed XML, or files with keys in comments all pass validation incorrectly.
contributing_factors: No XML parser imported (should use plistlib or xml.etree.ElementTree)
No exception handling for file read errors
No validation that Info.plist is actually a valid plist file
String-based key detection is fragile (keys could be in comments, values, or partial matches)
execution_path: Checks if privacy keys exist in text (line 485)
Returns passed=True even if file is empty/malformed
root_cause_analyses.impact: False positive privacy compliance. Apps with missing or malformed Info.plist privacy keys will pass pre-submission testing, leading to certain App Store rejection under Guideline 5.1.1.

root_cause_analyses.proximate_cause: swift_file.read_text() without encoding parameter or exception handling
root_cause_analyses.root_cause: All 6 Swift scanning methods assume all .swift files are valid UTF-8 text. Python's read_text() uses default encoding (platform-dependent, usually UTF-8) but will raise UnicodeDecodeError on files with different encoding, binary content, or corruption. The outer try-except (line 360, 408, etc.) catches Exception but the error message 'failed: <error>' doesn't help users identify which file caused the problem.
contributing_factors: No per-file exception handling to identify problematic files
No graceful degradation (skip bad files and continue)
Error messages don't include file paths
No validation that file is actually text before reading
execution_path: Exception caught at line 386, returns generic 'failed' message
User cannot identify which file caused failure
root_cause_analyses.impact: Pre-submission review crashes on projects with non-standard Swift files. Users get unhelpful error messages and cannot identify or fix the problematic file. Entire review fails instead of skipping bad files.

root_cause_analyses.proximate_cause: No validation of project.name before generating app name (line 155)
root_cause_analyses.root_cause: App name generation assumes project.name is always valid, non-empty, and meaningful. The _generate_app_name() method truncates to MAX_NAME_LENGTH (30 chars) but never checks for minimum length, empty strings, or whitespace-only names. This violates App Store requirement that app names must be 1-30 characters and cannot be empty or generic.
contributing_factors: No stripping of whitespace from project.name
Test fixtures likely all have valid names, masking the bug
execution_path: If name is empty/whitespace, returns empty/whitespace
Returns invalid app name to AppStoreAssets
App Store submission rejected for invalid name
root_cause_analyses.impact: App Store metadata generation produces invalid app names for projects with empty or malformed names. Submission will be rejected under App Store Review Guideline 2.3.7 (accurate metadata required).
